Welcome to News App’s documentation!¶

Modules¶

Views¶

class news_app1.views.ArticleViewSet(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelViewSet

API ViewSet for Article CRUD operations with role-based filtering.

filter_backends = [<class 'rest_framework.filters.SearchFilter'>]¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Filters API results based on authentication and user role.

perform_create(serializer)[source]¶

Ensures article authors are set to the current user via API.

search_fields = ['id', 'title']¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news_app1.views.CustomUserCreationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: UserCreationForm

A custom registration form that extends Django’s UserCreationForm.

Includes a role selection field for Readers, Journalists, and Editors.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: Meta

fields = ('username', 'role')¶
model¶

alias of User

base_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}¶
declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news_app1.views.NewsletterViewSet(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelViewSet

API ViewSet for Newsletter operations.

queryset = <QuerySet [<Newsletter: The Deep-Dive>, <Newsletter: TEST1>, <Newsletter: TEST>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of NewsletterSerializer

class news_app1.views.PublisherCreationForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

A model form for Editors to create new Publisher entities.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['name']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

base_fields = {'name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

news_app1.views.api_approved_log(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to log approval events.

news_app1.views.approve_article_action(request, article_id)[source]¶

Provides a quick POST action for Editors to approve an article.

news_app1.views.approve_newsletter_action(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Provides a quick POST action for Editors to approve a newsletter.

news_app1.views.article_detail_view(request, article_id)[source]¶

Displays the full content of a specific news article.

news_app1.views.author_article_list(request, author_id)[source]¶

Displays a public page showing articles from a specific author.

news_app1.views.create_article_view(request)[source]¶

Displays a form for Journalists to submit new articles.

news_app1.views.create_newsletter_view(request)[source]¶

Displays a form for Journalists to create a newsletter from approved articles.

news_app1.views.create_publisher_view(request)[source]¶

Displays a form for Editors to create new Publisher organizations.

news_app1.views.custom_404_view(request, exception)[source]¶

Returns a user-friendly custom 404 Error page.

news_app1.views.delete_article_view(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows a Journalist to delete their own article.

news_app1.views.delete_newsletter_view(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Allows the author or an Editor to delete a specific newsletter.

news_app1.views.edit_article_view(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows a Journalist to edit their own article, resetting approval status.

news_app1.views.edit_newsletter_view(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Allows Journalists or Editors to modify a newsletter’s content.

news_app1.views.editor_approval_list(request)[source]¶

Displays a dashboard of all articles and newsletters awaiting approval.

news_app1.views.editor_edit_review(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows an Editor to review, edit, and leave notes on a specific article.

news_app1.views.editor_newsletter_list(request)[source]¶

Displays a comprehensive list of all newsletters for administrative management.

news_app1.views.home_landing_page(request)[source]¶

Root view providing the correct portal content based on user role.

news_app1.views.journalist_article_list(request)[source]¶

Displays a management list of all articles written by the Journalist.

news_app1.views.journalist_directory(request)[source]¶

Displays a public directory of system Journalists for Readers to follow.

news_app1.views.journalist_newsletter_list(request)[source]¶

Displays a list of newsletters created by the logged-in Journalist.

news_app1.views.newsletter_list_view(request)[source]¶

Displays a list of all approved newsletters to the public.

news_app1.views.publisher_article_list(request, publisher_id)[source]¶

Displays a public page showing articles from a specific Publisher.

news_app1.views.publisher_directory(request)[source]¶

Displays a public directory of Publishers for Readers to follow.

news_app1.views.register_user(request)[source]¶

Handles new user registration and automatic login.

news_app1.views.subscribed_articles_view(request)[source]¶

Displays a feed of approved articles from the user’s subscriptions.

news_app1.views.toggle_subscription(request, target_type, target_id)[source]¶

Toggles a Reader’s subscription to a Journalist or Publisher.

Models¶

class news_app1.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Represents a news article submitted by a Journalist.

Contains content, approval status, and feedback notes from Editors.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

included_in_newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

class news_app1.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A curated collection of approved articles created by a Journalist.

Requires Editor approval before becoming visible to Readers.

class news_app1.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Represents a publishing organization.

Links Editors and Journalists to a specific publication entity.

class news_app1.models.User(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom User model extending AbstractUser to include role-based access.

Roles include Reader, Journalist, and Editor. This model handles subscription relationships for Readers and automatically manages Django Group assignments based on the chosen role.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = (('Reader', 'Reader'), ('Journalist', 'Journalist'), ('Editor', 'Editor'))¶

In the example:

class Child(Model):

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Overrides the save method to handle group assignment and field cleanup based on the user’s role.
